Perry Ehimuh
832-341-8762 | PerryEhimuh@gmail.com |
 https://www.linkedin.com/in/perry-ehimuh/ | https://github.com/PerryE25
Education
The University of Texas at Austin | Austin, TX                                                                                                         December 2026
Bachelor of Science | Computer Science                                                                                                                
•	Relevant Coursework: Data Structures and Algorithms, Discrete Mathematics, Introduction to Programming, Linear Algebra, Introduction to Python

Technical Skills
Programing Languages: Java, Python, C, HTML/CSS, and JavaScript
Frameworks/Libraries/Tools: Visual Studio Code, Eclipse, GitHub, Git, Linux, Bash, Microsoft Office, Adobe Creative Suite, Photography, Outlook, IntelliJ, React.js, Project Management
Certificates: Harvard CS50’s AP Computer Science Principles (2020), University of Houston Downtown Scholars Academy Certificate of Completion (2018)

Experience
Mobalytics Gaming Development Internship | Remote                                                                    June 2024 – July 2024
Mobalytics Gaming
•	Conducted comprehensive market research on gaming genres, analyzing game mechanics, player behavior, and competitive scenes.
•	Developed detailed insights on player profiles and game performance, including in-game decision-making and community dynamics.
•	Presented actionable recommendations through a 7-10 slide presentation, summarizing research findings and suggesting strategic expansions for Mobalytics.
Athletics Creative Development Internship | Austin, TX                                                   October 2021 – December 2021
University of Texas at Austin
•	In charge of creating media for UT’s athletic teams through photography, videography, graphic design, and analyzing social media data for such teams.
Self-Employed Photographer | Houston, TX                                                                                January 2021 – May 2021
•	Led my own business participating in sports photography, portraits, graduation pictures, etc.
•	In charge of the online distribution of pictures under the business management of “Mr. E’s Media”
•	Photography Portfolio

Projects
  Multi-Instance SaaS App with KubePlus & GKE  | BitBucket                                                                                July 2025
   Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), KubePlus, Helm, Bash CLI, REST, NetworkPolicy
•	Utilized KubePlus to register Helm chart as a Kubernetes-native Custom Resource (CRD), enabling kubectl-based deployment of multiple tenant-isolated namespaces with network policies and dynamic resource provisioning.
•	• Extended app logic with a /metrics endpoint to track usage stats for each HTTP route; built kubectl-appmetrics-show CLI plugin to extract live instance-level telemetry via REST over custom kubeconfig.
•	• Packaged Helm chart with versioned .tgz, deployed production-grade containers to GCR for GKE cluster consumption, and parameterized tenant configs (e.g., GREETING, NODE_PORT, IMAGE) for scalable instance rollout.        
Helm Chart Deployment & CLI Tooling – “Greetings” Microservice | BitBucket                                                   July 2025
   Helm, Kubernetes (Minikube), RBAC, Bash CLI, YAML, REST APIs
•	Engineered a reusable Helm chart to deploy a containerized Python microservice across multiple namespaces (greetings1, greetings2), using dynamic Helm variables for replicas, ports, and environment configs.
•	• Enhanced application endpoints (/greetings, /listcontents, /getk8sobjects) with namespace-aware RBAC queries; passed current namespace via environment variable using Helm’s .Release.Namespace.
•	• Built custom kubectl CLI plugins (greetings-show, greetings-delete) in Bash to automate service introspection and release deletion via REST and Helm API calls.         
Kubernetes App Orchestration– “Greetings” Service | BitBucket                                                                            June 2025
  Kubernetes (Minikube, kubectl), YAML, RBAC, ConfigMaps
•	Deployed a containerized Python app from Docker to a local Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, implementing deployment specs with 3-replica pods, environment variables, and volume mounts.
•	Defined and configured Kubernetes Service (NodePort), ServiceAccount, ConfigMap, and RBAC (Roles/RoleBindings) using greetings.yaml, applying correct selectors and labels for resource management.
•	Connected local Docker CLI to Minikube’s Docker daemon to build and push custom images; validated app behavior using kubectl and curl commands for testing pods, services, and mounted volumes.      
Docker Web App Deployment – “Greetings” Service | BitBucket                                                                            June 2025
  Docker, Shell Scripting, Python (Flask)
•	Built and containerized a Python web service with Docker using a custom Ubuntu 22.04 base image, environment variables, and volume mounting to support runtime configuration.
•	Engineered RESTful endpoints (/greetings, /listcontents) to return environment-based greetings and host volume contents dynamically; exposed app via port 5001.
•	Scripted Docker image build and run automation using Bash (build.sh, updated-run.sh), enabling flexible runtime behavior through parameterized execution.     
Collatz Problem Solver with Optimization| GitLab                                                                                                   June 2025
   Python, GitLab CI, cProfile, Unit Testing, Optimization
•	Implemented the 3n+1 Collatz Conjecture in Python with memoization caching for performance optimization, achieving 100% test coverage and successful GitLab CI integration.
•	Developed and ran unit tests and acceptance tests (100-200 lines) to cover edge cases, achieving robust code reliability and debugging with cProfile.
•	Collaborated efficiently with a partner, making regular commits, managing 15+ GitLab issues, and submitting optimized code to Sphere Online Judge after resolving performance bottlenecks.   
TXFishSpots Web App IDB (Coddle)| GitLab                                                                                                          June 2025
  Flask, React, SQLAlchemy, MySQL, Postman, GCP, Bootstrap, Python, JavaScript, GitLab CI
•	• Designed and implemented a dynamic web application emulating IMDB to track Texas Fish Spots with three interlinked models using Flask, SQLAlchemy, and React.
•	Developed the front-end using React, creating the Home and About Me pages, integrating Flipcards with CSS for interactive design, and building a Developer Tools carousel using a wrap-around array. Also made a 30-second video using CapCut for user experience.
•	Collaborated on GitLab, using version control, handling 15+ issues, creating unit tests, and contributing to a detailed technical report and presentation.      
  Snake Arcade Game | GitHub                                                                                                                              January 2023
  HTML/CSS/JavaScript, P5.js Libraries
•	Utilized JavaScript classes to engineer the 1976 arcade snake game; pioneered a snake class that eats food and grows snake body with array shifting/traversal algorithm
•	Used scale algorithm to update resolutions correspondingly
•	Organized technical workshop to administrate front-end engineering concepts to 10 undergraduates.
  Image to ASCII Filter | GitHub                                                                                                                        November 2022
  HTML/CSS/JavaScript, P5.js Libraries
•	Engineered a live processes video converting each pixel to ASCII text corresponding to each individual pixel’s brightness value; calculated brightness values from average of RGB values
•	Utilized P5.js Libraries to create a multidimensional array-traversal algorithm to map pixels to black and white characters
•	Organized technical workshop to administrate front-end engineering concepts to 15 undergraduates
  Sudoku Solver | GitHub                                                                                                                                     October 2022
  Python, PyGames API 
•	Implemented a recursive backtracking algorithm to design a sudoku solver that takes player’s input and can auto play
•	Simulated and computed the player’s tries utilizing PyGames API
  Directly | GitHub                                                                                                                                                  October 2022
  HTML/CSS & JavaScript, TopSites API, Commands API
•	Pioneered the creation of a Google Chrome shortcut extension from a client’s input and automated recommended shortcuts after 3 days of use using users’ requests adhering to the TopSites API
•	Won 3rd Place in Hack TX 2022 using UI/UX and ideation workshops
  YouTube to Spotify Automation | GitHub                                                                                                      September 2022
  Python, YouTube Data API, Spotify Web API, Python Requests API
•	Leveraged Python to automate the creation of a Spotify Playlist from a user’s Liked Video playlist on YouTube using HTTP requests adhering to the Spotify Web API
•	Organized playlists on average of 30 to 100 songs and converted to Spotify
•	Analyzed video data from the Liked Videos playlist of a YouTube account using the YouTube Data API to determine the contents of the HTTP request
  Connect Four Simulation | GitHub                                                                                                                             April 2022
  Java, Scanner API
•	Created a Connect Four simulation using Java array-traversal algorithms
•	Used data structures, multi-dimensional array traversing, and two-player user input
  Hangman | GitHub
•	Developed the Hangman game with conditionals, standard input, and loops learned in Intro to Programming course
  Decipher | GitHub
•	Used frequency analysis to initially decrypt a secret message with tallying algorithm
•	Swapped letters around two-by-two as a detective to get final decrypted message
  University of Houston Downtown (UHD) | Prototype Website
•	Prototyped a sample website for UHD with a small group of 20 programmers
•	Oversaw the Texas Workforce Commission’s 2018 General Statement in my subgroup of three people
•	Created the design of my subgroup’s webpage and inputting the information for each person’s box of text.
  HackTX 2021
•	Attended workshops and understood the project-building aspects of technology at the University of Texas at Austin’s annual hackathon
•	Learned Python, GitHub, Ideation, and sharpened my interview skills

Leadership & Community Involvement
Association of Black Computer Scientists (ABCS) | Austin, TX	December 2021 – Present
Vice President | Internal Outreach Officer
•	Promoted technical projects to dozens of members and guided the Computer Science internal transfer program
•	Organized members into meetings associated with Tech companies, promoted diversity around the University of Texas Computer Science (UTCS) program, and uplifted mental health in UTCS program.
National Society of Black Engineers | Austin, TX	           October 2021 – Present
NSBE Constituent
•	Increased the number of culturally responsible African American Engineers by roughly 10%
•	Represented engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community
Texas Convergent | Austin, TX	October 2021 – Present
Member
•	Organized projects among Business and Computer Science students and took initiative in project presenting
ABCS LBJ Mentorship Program | Austin, TX	           October 2022 – Present
•	Volunteered | 15 hours
•	Led group of 30 students in the LBJ High School mentorship programming project of providing information about UTCS opportunities and provided internships for 5 students while in high school.

Educational Courses
Software Engineering | University of Texas at Austin	     June 2025 – July 2025
CS Upper-Division Elective
•	Developed full-stack web applications using Flask, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, and deployed them to the cloud via Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Namecheap.
•	Built and tested Python modules using unit testing, code coverage, and continuous integration with GitLab CI and Makefiles.
•	Collaborated on 2 major software projects, each emphasizing different aspects of software engineering: testing, cloud deployment, database integration, and RESTful APIs.
•	Learned modern development workflows, including version control with Git/GitLab, code documentation with pydoc/pdoc, and pair programming techniques.
•	Explored agile methodologies through weekly readings from Extreme Programming Installed and authored technical papers on topics such as team collaboration, CI, and interviewing strategies.
•	Practiced API design and documentation using Postman, and implemented secure client-server communication protocols.
•	Gained hands-on experience with React, HTML/CSS/JS, and frontend-backend integration for dynamic web applications.
•	Authored 7 programming assignments and a technical report, refining academic writing skills in line with the course’s Writing Flag designation.
•	Designed and normalized relational databases with UML diagrams, ER diagrams, and practiced SQL joins, queries, and schema mapping.
•	Demonstrated technical work during the final weeks, demonstrating knowledge of the software development lifecycle from conception to deployment. 
Cloud Native Computing | University of Texas at Austin	             June 2025 – July 2025 CS Upper-Division Elective
•	• Deployed containerized applications using Docker, authoring Dockerfiles, managing volumes, and following industry-standard container best practices.
•	• Orchestrated microservices and workloads with Kubernetes, working with Minikube, kubectl, YAML configurations, StatefulSets, ConfigMaps, and RoleBindings.
•	• Automated Kubernetes application deployments with Helm, creating and customizing Helm charts for real-world use cases.
•	• Implemented multi-tenancy and SaaS application patterns using KubePlus, exploring namespace isolation, RBAC, and cloud-native operator design.
•	• Monitored Kubernetes clusters using Prometheus, configuring observability stacks for metric collection, alerting, and performance tracking.
•	• Completed 4 major programming assignments and 3 quizzes covering cloud-native architecture, container orchestration, CI/CD, and logging/monitoring practices.
•	• Gained practical knowledge of CNCF tools and contributed to class discussions on open-source workflows and Kubernetes community contributions.

Honors & Awards
TAFE Educators Rising Ethical Dilemma Varsity 1st Place National Qualifier Scholarship (April 2021)
•	Led my group of three people against the dozens of other Ethical Dilemma competitors in the state of Texas.
•	Created solutions that teachers encounter in the Texas Association of Future Educators (TAFE),
•	Earned first place in the state of Texas and moved on to top five in the Nation.   Dell Scholars (University of Texas at Austin)
•	Received grant for a Dell laptop and textbook credit

Additional Information
Interests: Software Engineering, Web Development, UI/UX Design, working out, basketball, photography, video editing, video games, activism, philosophy, motivation, Unix, Framework, Encryption, Artificial Intelligence, E-Commerce
Work Eligibility: Eligible to work with no restrictions in the U.S.
Soft Skills: Self-Motivated, Open-mindedness, Effective Communication, Critical Thinking, Adaptable

Things to add: Haven Men Exec, ABCS VP, Canes crewmember 2 months, Kumon teaching assistant 5 months, Kylan’s automation project

========================
PRE-WRITTEN APPLICATION ANSWERS
===============================

Why are you interested in this company?
I’m interested in this company because of its focus on building impactful technology at scale and its strong engineering culture. I’m especially drawn to opportunities where I can contribute to real-world products while continuing to grow my skills in software engineering, cloud systems, and full-stack development. I’m excited about the possibility of working on meaningful problems and learning from experienced engineers.

Why are you interested in this role?
I’m interested in this role because it aligns with my background in software engineering and my interest in building scalable applications. Through my coursework and projects, I’ve worked with full-stack technologies, cloud platforms, and Kubernetes, and I’m excited to apply these skills in a real-world engineering environment while continuing to learn and grow.

Why are you a good fit?
I’m a strong fit because I bring a combination of technical skills, hands-on project experience, and a willingness to learn quickly. I’ve built full-stack applications and worked with modern tools like Kubernetes, Docker, and React, and I’m comfortable adapting to new technologies. I’m also highly motivated and collaborative, and I’m looking for an environment where I can contribute while continuing to grow as an engineer.

Describe a technical project:
One of my most impactful projects was building a multi-instance SaaS application using Kubernetes and Google Kubernetes Engine. I implemented a system that allows multiple isolated deployments using Helm and custom resource definitions, and added a metrics endpoint to track usage across instances. This project helped me understand cloud-native architecture, scalability, and how to design systems for real-world deployment.

Career goals (2–3 years):
In the next 2–3 years, my goal is to grow into a strong software engineer by gaining experience working on production systems and collaborating with engineering teams. I also plan to pursue a master’s degree to deepen my technical knowledge and eventually move into higher-level engineering roles where I can contribute to system design and architecture.

Preferred programming language:
My preferred programming languages are Java and JavaScript. I enjoy Java for its structure and use in backend systems, and JavaScript for building interactive front-end applications. Together, they allow me to work across the full stack.
